channel.title: Combined Politepol Feed
channel.description: Merged feed from multiple Politepol sources

item.title: 2 weeks of Iran war: Major takeaways as peace remains out of bounds
Inside West Asia’s escalating war: How will it end? An expert explains...
Why are Oscar statuettes are only worth $1?
Will US attacks on Iran’s Kharg Island push up oil prices?
This Week in Explainers: Why Iran war has sent Indians scrambling for LPG cylinders
Iran war: How a surge in oil prices could hit India’s economy
How Hormuz crisis exposes global complacency on maritime chokepoints
item.description: Energy crises often spark talk of diversification and strategic reserves, but prices eventually stabilise and supply routes resume. Markets call this risk, history calls it a habit

item.title: How India can act as a ‘peace architect’ in West Asia
item.description: India’s foreign policy framework—built on strong, simultaneous ties with Israel, Iran, the US, and Gulf nations—positions it as a potential ‘back‑channel’ negotiator and peace architect in West Asia

item.title: How French archives in Pondicherry are helping India recover stolen temple idols
item.description: The decades-old photographic archives from two French institutes documenting South Indian temples have become critical evidence in proving the provenance of stolen idols and helping India bring its lost antiquities home

item.title: The mirage of Pax Sinica and Pax Americana: How India’s strategic autonomy paves the way for a multipolar world
item.description: India offers a values-based vision for humankind. Its strategic autonomy has served it well and positions it to rise, offering the world choices beyond the binary of Pax Americana or Pax Sinica

item.title: How Israel could emerge as the only winner in the Iran war
item.description: Israel appears to be the only player that entered the conflict with a clear objective, and the only one that may derive any tangible gains from it

item.title: India’s West Asia playbook: The art of not taking sides
item.description: As the sands of West Asia continue to shift, India’s neutral hand and unblinking focus on its own national interest will remain its greatest diplomatic asset
